mission.title: kserve: Protect KFServing endpoint
mission.description: Protect KFServing endpoint. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current kserve deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kserve version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
helm list -n kserve 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kserve installation.

steps.title: Review kserve configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kserve configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get configmap -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kserve
```
Currently, a `kfserving-gateway` is used for kfserving inference endpoints. It uses LoadBalancer and all endpoints are publicly available without protection. 

This doesn't meet requirement for production grade cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Protect KFServing endpoint
steps.description:
The story is a bit complicated but I think there is no behavior change as far as I know for Istio/Dex. I do not think KFServing ever worked in Kubeflow with Auth due to various issues we found along the way.

- KFServing was initially shipped in Kubeflow 0.7 with KFServing 0.2.0(which is later
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: kfserving-kubeflow-yuzi-dan-kfserving-test
  namespace: kubeflow-yuzi-dan
spec:
  gateways:
  - kubeflow/kubeflow-gateway
  hosts:
  - '*'
  http:
  - match:
    - uri:
        prefix: /kfserving/kubeflow-yuzi-dan/sklearn-iris
    rewrite:
        uri: /v1/models/sklearn-iris:predict
    route:
    - destination:
        host: cluster-local-gateway.istio-system.svc.cluster.local
      headers:
        request:
          set:
            Host: sklearn-iris-predictor-default.kubeflow-yuzi-dan.svc.cluster.local
      
```

steps.title: Upgrade kserve to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kserve:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kserve kserve/kserve --namespace kserve
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
helm list -n kserve
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get events -n kserve --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Protect KFServing endpoint" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The story is a bit complicated but I think there is no behavior change as far as I know for Istio/Dex. I do not think KFServing ever worked in Kubeflow with Auth due to various issues we found along the way.

- KFServing was initially shipped in Kubeflow 0.7 with KFServing 0.2.0(which is later patched with 0.2.2 for pod mutator issue in 0.7.1)  and Knative 0.8.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: kfserving-kubeflow-yuzi-dan-kfserving-test
  namespace: kubeflow-yuzi-dan
spec:
  gateways:
  - kubeflow/kubeflow-gateway
  hosts:
  - '*'
  http:
  - match:
    - uri:
        prefix: /kfserving/kubeflow-yuzi-dan/sklearn-iris
    rewrite:
        uri: /v1/models/sklearn-iris:predict
    route:
    - destination:
        host: cluster-local-gateway.istio-system.svc.cluster.local
      headers:
        request:
          set:
            Host: sklearn-iris-predictor-default.kubeflow-yuzi-dan.svc.cluster.local
      weight: 100
    timeout: 300s

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kserve installed or the issue environment reproducible.
